key_insight: Pulsed plasma treatment densifies Fe–TiB2–CrB2 detonation coatings and boosts near-surface hardness, wear resistance, and corrosion resistance without changing the main phase set.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical post-treatment route to upgrade wear- and corrosion-critical coatings for demanding surface engineering applications.
method: Pulsed plasma treatment (PPT) of the coating surface
XRD, microscopy, microhardness, tribology, and electrochemical testing
evidence_signal: The abstract reports retention of α-Fe, TiB2, and CrB2 phases, a 15–20% microhardness increase, reduced wear depth, and a 0.15–0.20 V positive corrosion-potential shift after PPT.
watch_item: Benefits appear concentrated in the near-surface zone, so process control is important to avoid nonuniform treatment or property gradients across the coating.
paper_title: STUDIES OF THE STRUCTURAL AND PHASE STATE OF DETONATION COATINGS BASED ON THE FE–TIB₂–CRB₂ EUTECTIC SYSTEM AFTERPULSE PLASMA TREATMENT
